description_long: This object is a compact, metallic device with a rectangular central body and four cylindrical extensions, two on each side, resembling connection ports or barrels. The central section features a raised, rectangular block with a prominent hexagonal bolt on top, suggesting a mechanical or functional component. The surface is uniformly metallic with a matte, slightly rough finish, and the overall construction appears robust and industrial. The cylindrical extensions are symmetrically arranged, with chamfered edges and visible seams, indicating precision machining. The object lacks any visible trigger, stock, or magazine, and does not display typical ergonomic features of a handheld rifle. Its design is more reminiscent of a mechanical or hydraulic component than a conventional firearm.
description: A compact, metallic, rectangular device with four cylindrical extensions and a central raised block with a hexagonal bolt. The surface is matte and industrial, lacking typical rifle features.
description_view_0: Shows the top and two adjacent cylindrical extensions, highlighting the central raised block and hexagonal bolt.
description_view_1: Displays the side and bottom, emphasizing the solid, featureless side and the alignment of the cylindrical extensions.
description_view_2: Reveals the underside and the parallel arrangement of the cylindrical extensions, with the central block visible from the side.
description_view_3: Focuses on the ends of the cylindrical extensions, showing their hollow interiors and the angular body shape.
